summary: Fail. The repo now has an ordinary-program loop example and related scenario coverage, but it is not yet honestly swarm-ready under the full task goal because the primary orchestration model still depends on shell wrappers and the Clasp-side swarm API is still a thin stringly wrapper over `claspc swarm`/`@swarm`, not a typed Clasp-native substrate.
findings: Builder/verifier orchestration still relies on shell wrapper scripts as a primary path. `scripts/clasp-codex-loop.sh`, `scripts/clasp-codex-loop-start.sh`, `scripts/clasp-codex-loop-status.sh`, `scripts/clasp-codex-loop-stop.sh`, and `scripts/test-codex-loop.sh` remain the full live loop workflow with start/status/stop behavior. That does not satisfy the task direction to make the system startable as an ordinary Clasp program without requiring shell wrapper orchestration.

The new ordinary-program swarm path is still CLI-shaped and stringly at the Clasp layer. In `examples/swarm-native/Swarm.clasp`, every operation is implemented via `runCommandJson` with `@swarm` commands and returns `Result Str` JSON text, so real Clasp orchestrators still have to build command arrays and traffic in serialized JSON instead of using typed Clasp-native control-plane/state APIs. That is still scaffold-level for a serious self-improving swarm implemented in Clasp itself.

The repository therefore does not yet clear the acceptance bar that there be no obvious missing substrate forcing real orchestration logic back out into shell/CLI glue. Ordinary-program execution exists, durable native state exists, and scenario coverage exists for those slices, but the end-to-end model is still centered on shell scripts plus CLI command wrapping rather than a genuinely Clasp-native orchestration surface.
tests_run: Reviewed task-related diffs against `.clasp-loops/swarm-ready-full-goal-live/baseline-workspace`, especially `examples/feedback-loop/Main.clasp`, `runtime/swarm.rs`, and `scripts/test-native-claspc.sh`.
Inspected current orchestration paths in `scripts/clasp-codex-loop*.sh`, `examples/swarm-native/Main.clasp`, `examples/swarm-native/Swarm.clasp`, and `runtime/clasp_runtime.rs` (`@swarm` handling).
follow_up: Replace the shell-script codex loop with an ordinary Clasp program that owns the live builder/verifier workflow, including operational control surfaces, rather than keeping `scripts/clasp-codex-loop*.sh` as the primary path.

Expose typed Clasp-native swarm/control-plane APIs for objectives, tasks, leases, approvals, runs, artifacts, and merge decisions instead of requiring `runCommandJson` with `@swarm` plus JSON-string plumbing.
Once the architecture is genuinely Clasp-native end to end, rerun repo-wide verification with `bash scripts/verify-all.sh`.
